title: C++ Output (cout)
content:
### Console Output with cout

The `cout` object, along with the insertion operator `<<`, is used to display output to the console.

---

### Basic Output

```cpp
#include <iostream>
using namespace std;

int main() {
  cout << "Hello World!";
  return 0;
}
```

---

### Multiple Items

You can chain multiple `<<` operators to output different data types:

```cpp
cout << "The value is " << 42 << " and pi is " << 3.14 << endl;
```

---

### New Lines

| Method | Code | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| endl | `cout << "Line 1" << endl;` | Flushes buffer, adds newline |
| \\n | `cout << "Line 1\\n";` | Newline character only |

---

### Formatting Output

```cpp
#include <iomanip>

cout << fixed << setprecision(2) << 3.14159;  // Output: 3.14
cout << setw(10) << "right";                   // Right-aligned in 10 spaces
```

---

> **Key Point:** Use `endl` to end a line and flush the output buffer. Use `\\n` when you only need a newline without flushing.

quiz.question: Which operator is used with cout to output data?
